E6-FM-01: require the model to list assumptions before recommendation writing
use prompts that ask what must be true for this model to hold
E6-FM-02: require at least two competing metrics or stakeholder effects
use visible tradeoff matrices before final modeling
E6-FM-03: require one public-policy or institutional condition in the explanation
ask what rule subsidy tax or constraint changes the economic recommendation
E6-FM-04: simplify representations before full quantitative or qualitative comparison
verify whether representation scaffolds materially change performance
